_description: Maps stains that have common combo variants to a follow-up question + variant routing. When user selects a trigger stain, the app shows a follow-up chip row BEFORE proceeding to surface selection. Each variant routes to the correct protocol type.

coffee.reason: Black coffee is a pure tannin stain. Coffee with cream/milk adds protein + fat, requiring a combination protocol (treat protein FIRST at low temp, then tannin).

variants.label: Black coffee
variants.notes: Routes to standard coffee protocols (coffee-{surface}.json). Tannin-only treatment.

variants.label: With cream or milk
variants.notes: coffee-cream-any.json exists as universal. Surface-specific combo protocols needed. Treat protein first (cold water enzyme), then tannin (acid/oxidizer).

variants.label: Latte / Cappuccino
variants.notes: Same chemistry as coffee w/ cream — high milk ratio makes protein component dominant. Route to coffee-cream protocols.

tea.reason: Black/green/herbal tea is a pure tannin stain. Tea with milk adds protein requiring combination treatment. Chai with milk and spices adds protein + oil.

variants.label: Black / green / herbal
variants.notes: Routes to standard tea protocols (tea-{surface}.json or tea+{surface}.json). Pure tannin treatment.

variants.label: With milk
variants.notes: MISSING — needs creation. Same combination treatment logic as coffee-cream: cold protein removal first, then tannin treatment.

variants.label: Chai latte
variants.notes: MISSING — needs creation. Protein + tannin + trace oil from spices. Route to tea-milk protocols.

hot chocolate.reason: Hot chocolate is ALWAYS a combination stain (cocoa tannin + dairy protein + cocoa butter fat). No follow-up needed — auto-route to combo protocol.

variants.label: Hot chocolate
variants.notes: hot-chocolate-berber-carpet.json and hot-chocolate-wool-sweater.json exist. Need broader surface coverage. Always combo: protein (casein) + tannin (cocoa polyphenols) + fat (cocoa butter). Treat fat first (surfactant/solvent), then protein (cold enzyme), then tannin.

chocolate.reason: Dark chocolate is primarily tannin + fat (no dairy). Milk chocolate adds dairy protein, requiring combination treatment sequence. White chocolate is fat-only.

variants.label: Dark / plain chocolate
variants.notes: Routes to existing chocolate-{surface}.json protocols. Tannin + cocoa butter. Treat fat first (dish soap/solvent), then tannin (oxidizer).

variants.label: Milk chocolate
variants.notes: chocolate-milk-baby-clothes.json and chocolate-milk-car-carpet.json exist. Needs broader coverage. Triple combo: dairy protein + cocoa tannin + fat.

variants.label: White chocolate
variants.notes: MISSING — needs creation. No cocoa solids = no tannin. Pure fat stain (cocoa butter + dairy fat). Treat as oil/grease stain.

sauce.reason: Tomato-only sauce is tannin + lycopene (dye). Meat sauce adds protein + animal fat, requiring combination treatment. Cream sauce is protein + fat.

variants.label: Tomato / marinara
variants.notes: Routes to existing tomato-sauce-{surface}.json protocols. Tannin + lycopene dye component. Flush cold, then enzyme + oxidizer.

variants.label: Meat sauce / bolognese
variants.notes: MISSING — needs creation. Protein (meat) + tannin (tomato) + animal fat. Cold rinse for protein first, then degrease, then tannin treatment.

variants.label: Cream / alfredo
variants.notes: MISSING — needs creation. Dairy protein + butter fat. No tannin component. Cold enzyme first, then degrease.

variants.label: Soy sauce
variants.notes: Existing soy-sauce protocols handle this. Strong tannin + melanoidin dye. Similar to tomato but darker oxidized pigment.

salad dressing.reason: Vinaigrette is primarily oil + acid (no protein). Creamy dressings (ranch, caesar, blue cheese) add dairy protein and emulsifiers, changing the treatment approach.

variants.label: Vinaigrette / oil-based
variants.notes: Routes to existing salad-dressing-{surface}.json protocols. Primarily oil with trace acid/vinegar. Standard degreasing treatment.

variants.label: Ranch / creamy
variants.notes: ranch-dressing-car-seat.json exists. Needs broader surface coverage. Dairy protein + oil emulsion. Cold enzyme for protein, then degrease.

variants.label: Caesar / blue cheese
variants.notes: MISSING — needs creation. Same chemistry as ranch (dairy + oil) plus anchovy/cheese protein. Route to ranch-dressing if caesar-specific doesn't exist.

_implementationNotes.triggerDetection: When user selects a stain from the chip row, check if stain name (lowercased, trimmed) matches a key in triggers{}. Also match sub-stain names: 'Coffee w/ Cream' should auto-select the cream variant, 'Black Coffee' should auto-select the black variant.
_implementationNotes.subStainMapping: Some sub-stains in SECTORS already distinguish variants (e.g. clothing sector has 'Black Coffee' and 'Coffee w/ Cream' as subs). For those, skip the follow-up and route directly. Only show follow-up when the user selected the generic parent (e.g. 'Coffee') without picking a sub.
_implementationNotes.autoRoute: If a trigger has autoRoute:true (hot chocolate), skip the follow-up question and route directly to the combo protocol.
_implementationNotes.surfaceAppend: Protocol file lookup: {routeTo}-{surface}.json. Example: user picks 'coffee-cream' + 'cotton' → look for coffee-cream-cotton.json. Fall back to coffee-cream-any.json if surface-specific doesn't exist.
_implementationNotes.chipRowPosition: Follow-up chips appear BETWEEN stain selection and surface selection, in a new row with the followUpQuestion as a label above. Same visual style as existing sub-stain chips.
_implementationNotes.protocolFallback: If no surface-specific combo protocol exists, fall through to the AI solve endpoint with the combo stain type tagged. The solve prompt already handles combination stains correctly.
_implementationNotes.existingSubStainInteraction: The clothing sector already has 'Coffee w/ Cream' and 'Black Coffee' as sub-stains under Coffee/Drink. For those, the existing sub-chip click should map directly to this routing table instead of needing a second follow-up. The follow-up system is for when the user types 'coffee' in search or picks the parent stain without expanding subs.
